title: Humanocentrism
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Citation}}
{{Quote|You refer to us as 'alien,' and the Princess calls us 'nonhuman.' Why are we defined by you and in comparison to you?|[[Borsk Fey'lya]] to [[Doman Beruss (Corellian)|Doman Beruss]], [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]|<ref name="X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble">''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]''</ref>}}
'''Humanocentrism''' was the tendency to view [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from the perspective of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] majority.

==History==
{{Quote|Well, you can never trust his kind, can you? I prefer dealing with humans, don't you?|[[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] to 12-year-old [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] regarding the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|security force]] leader's [[Blood Carver]] assassin, [[Ke Daiv]]—Skywalker's "first personal kill."|<ref name="Rogue Planet">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>}}
While more passive than [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]], Humanocentrism was pervasive. It was the source for the Human use of the term "[[Alien/Legends|alien]]" to describe nonhuman species and in some cases tended towards nonhuman [[speciesism]]. As such, the term is often synonymous with that variety of speciesism.

Proponents of extreme Humanocentrism pointed to the fact that Humans had founded the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]; many of the galaxy's best known military leaders and heroes were Humans; that the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] were almost all founded and universally inhabited by Humans; that [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Galactic capital, was widely believed to have been the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of mankind.

Although Humanocentrism was technically [[Anti-speciesism|outlawed]] by the [[Rights of Sentience]] clause of the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]], it still very much existed throughout the life of the Old Republic. [[Planet/Legends|Planets]] such as [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] reserved the best sections of the world for Humans, while forcing non-Humans into ghettos.

Although many of these infractions took place in areas of the Galaxy where the Republic had tenuous control at best (such as the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]), or no control (such as the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]), many of them existed where the Republic was very much in control—yet still refused to take action (the [[Avenue of the Core Founders/Legends|Avenue of the Core Founders]] being an example). At the time the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] broke out, there were still lawsuits pending about pro-Human bias in the Republic government.

[[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], the [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|CIS]] and [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] to [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], was a humanocentrist who sought to build an "Empire of Man" after deposing the alien separatist leaders and committing genocide against their species. He also viewed [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] modification on Humans as an abomination, and looked down on [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] for replacing his severed arm with a cybernetic prosthetic, believing that "a gentleman would have learned to fight one-handed."<ref name="ROTS Novel">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref>

While under the influence of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]], the [[Militarists/Legends|Militarist]] movement in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] became conflated with Humanocentrism, although the ideologies had apparently diverged by the time of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].

The idea of Humanocentrism reached its peak in the Human High Culture ideal of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and its subsequent genocide and [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. [[18 ABY/Legends|One year]] [[19 ABY/Legends|before]] the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|Empire's fall]], the [[Human League]] kept their ideals alive in New Republic space.

Aliens who were the victims of Humanocentrism or more overt anti-Alien speciesism sometimes reacted by developing their own [[anti-Humanism]]. A well-known example of this was the [[Diversity Alliance]].
